display.purpose: Hero and major brand statements only.

body.purpose: Protocol explanation and product credibility copy.

mono.purpose: Technical labels, contract stats, and proof-system metadata.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Large page choreography and nav transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary app launch and request-access action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; background: #F46F73; color: #FFFFFF; border: 0; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 1rem 2.5rem; font: 500 0.9375rem Manrope, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.04em; transition: background 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #CF5059; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 0 40px rgba(244, 111, 115, 0.15); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #F8F6F3; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Current repeated primitive card for protocol capabilities.
components.html:
ZK

Private Positions

Prove health without revealing size, direction, or strategy.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #F8F6F3; color: #262321; border: 1px solid rgba(147, 139, 133, 0.15); border-radius: 24px; padding: 2.5rem; max-width: 340px; font-family: Manrope, sans-serif; } .ds-card-icon { width: 48px; height: 48px; display: grid; place-items: center; border-radius: 12px; background: #F2EEEA; color: #F46F73; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; font: 400 0.875rem JetBrains Mono, monospace; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0 0 0.75rem; font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: #655D58; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Dark Email Field
components.css: .ds-email { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; color: #F8F6F3; font-family: Manrope, sans-serif; } .ds-email span { font-size: 0.8125rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-email input { background: rgba(248, 246, 243, 0.08); border: 1px solid rgba(248, 246, 243, 0.2); border-radius: 9999px; color: #F8F6F3; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; font-size: 0.9375rem; outline: none; } .ds-email input::placeholder { color: rgba(248, 246, 243, 0.6); } .ds-email input:focus { border-color: #F46F73; background: rgba(248, 246, 243, 0.05); }

narrative.overview: The current system presents Vayyl as a quiet, high-trust privacy protocol with editorial typography, warm dark surfaces, coral accents, and cinematic scroll motion. The design should evolve toward mechanism-led infrastructure: more proof path, less decorative mystique.
keyCharacteristics: Dark confidential hero surfaces with coral as the primary action color.
Warm off-white body sections for long-form technical explanation.
Serif display type for brand voice and Manrope for readable explanation.
GSAP-driven page choreography, with reduced-motion fallbacks preserved.
Diagrams and proof-flow visuals should carry more weight than generic card grids.

rules.name: The Coral Scarcity Rule
rules.body: Coral is strongest when rare. Use it for action and proof emphasis, not as ambient decoration.

rules.name: The No Privacy Fog Rule
rules.body: If a dark/glow treatment does not clarify privacy, proof, or settlement, remove it.

rules.name: The Mechanism Before Mood Rule
rules.body: Headlines can be poetic, but supporting copy must name the actual protocol mechanism.

rules.name: The Border Or Shadow Rule
rules.body: Do not pair a decorative 1px border with a wide soft shadow on the same card unless the state change earns it.

dos: Do lead with Vayyl's proof path: commitments, nullifiers, Merkle inclusion, Groth16, and Soroban BN254 verification.
Do keep reduced-motion behavior and ensure content is visible when animations are skipped.
Do use Proof Coral for primary action and proof emphasis only.
Do show testnet/project status honestly when making trust claims.
Do verify mobile text wrapping and CTA/input sizing on narrow screens.
donts: Don't ship copied SaaS landing-page templates with interchangeable hero copy and icon-card grids.
Don't use generic crypto neon, purple gradients, decorative glow orbs, or glassmorphism by default.
Don't make vague privacy claims without showing the mechanism.
Don't overclaim production readiness beyond current evidence.
Don't add repeated tiny uppercase tracked labels above every section.
Don't hide important content behind animation-only visibility states.
